macro_segments.title: Transition from Creation Timeline to Year-Designation Categories

micro_segments.title: Resolution of constellation and water-rising debate; consensus on Nisan as creation month
Unresolved tension between Nisan (computational) and Tishrei (liturgical) as world creation date
Return to the mishna's list of year-designations; introduction of vegetables (yerakot) for ma'aser

macro_segments.title: The Role of Vegetables in Ma'aser Determination: Prohibition of Cross-Year Tithes

micro_segments.title: Explanation of why vegetables establish year-boundaries for ma'aser; inability to tithe from one year onto the next
Two foundational questions by year-designation: calendar date (1 Tishrei) and determining event (e.g., birth timing for animals)

macro_segments.title: Exegesis of the Braita: Vegetables, Ma'asrot, and Vows as Year-Markers

micro_segments.title: Braita lists three categories: yerakot, ma'asrot, and vows; gemara asks why vegetables mentioned separately
Distinction between rabbinic ma'aser yerek and biblical ma'asrot; debate over whether fruit is biblical
Rashi vs. Tosafot: is ma'aser on fruit d'oraita or derabbanan? Tosafot eventually aligns with Rashi
Why mention yerakot first (derabbanan) before ma'asrot (d'oraita)? Rabbinical preference for their own decrees
Why does our mishna say only yerakot without mentioning ma'asrot generically? Avoidance of ambiguity with ma'aser behema
Plural yerakot alludes to two types of vegetables with different gmar melacha (completion of work) criteria
Bundled vegetables (completed when bundled) vs. unbundled vegetables (completed when vessel filled or harvest done)

macro_segments.title: The Picking Rule: Ma'aser Application Across Day Boundary

micro_segments.title: Braita: vegetables picked before sunset on Erev Rosh Hashanah and continued after sunset; cannot mix years
Even if violation of Yom Tov occurs (using non-Jewish worker), vegetables remain chayav in terumot u'ma'asrot
Proof that picking moment (before/after sunset) determines year assignment; cannot take teruma from new year on old or vice versa
Year-three to year-four transition: second-year produce gets ma'aser rishon & sheni; third-year gets ma'aser rishon & ani

macro_segments.title: The Ma'aser Ani in Years Three and Six: Source and Exclusion of Ma'aser Sheni

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi derives ma'aser ani (years 3–6) from Deuteronomy 'shnat ha'ma'aser'; gives to ger, yatom, almana
Gemara clarifies: 'shnat ha'ma'aser' means only one other tithe besides ma'aser ani; ma'aser sheni is suspended
Objection: maybe ma'aser rishon is also suspended? Resolved by 'va'el ha'Levi'im tedaber'—inheritance continuity ensures ma'aser rishon never stops
Second braita confirms: only ma'aser rishon & ani in year three; 'va'ba ha'Levi' shows Levi still receives tithe
Rabbi Eliezer ben Yaakov: proof from 'va'el ha'Levi'im' inheritance link alone suffices; no need for 'va'ba ha'Levi' verse

macro_segments.title: Vows and Year-Designation: Minimal Duration Suffices

micro_segments.title: Braita: vow of non-benefit from someone lasts 12 months from day to day; if vow specifies 'this year' it expires at 1 Tishrei
Even if vow taken on 29 Elul ('this year'), once 1 Tishrei arrives the year is complete and vow expires
Gemara: even those holding that 'one day in a year' is normally insufficient agree here because vow-making is self-affliction, achieved immediately

macro_segments.title: Stage-Based Year-Designation: Sprouting, Third-Ripening, and the Shelish Standard

micro_segments.title: Mishnah in Ma'asrot: taltan from sprouting; grain and olives from third-ripeness; implies different events trigger year-change for different plants
Gemara derives shelish (third-ripeness) from shmita-year grain usage: Deuteronomy 'shnat ha'shmita' at Sukkot implies grain harvested then had stage of shelish before Rosh Hashanah
Rav Zeira challenges: how do you know exactly shelish and not less? Answer: rabbis establish precise measures like mikvah's arba'im se'ah, egg-sized tumah
Law requires cutoff points; epsilon differences matter (e.g., arba'im se'ah minus thumbnail cannot immerse); thus shelish is established as the operative standard
Tosafot: all various stage-markers (sprouting, shelish, blossoming) are applications of the shelish principle, measurable by identifiable signs
Yerushalmi: taltan from sprouting (mishetitzmach) = seeds fit for planting; an application of shelish concept to fenugreek

topical_tags.term: Fruit vs. vegetable tithing status (biblical vs. rabbinic)
Rashi position (grain, olives, grapes only biblical)
Crossing year boundary (Erev Rosh Hashanah to Rosh Hashanah)
Cannot mix years in tithing (ein tormin u'ma'asrin)
Ma'aser sheni (second tithe, eaten in Jerusalem)
Shnat ha'ma'aser (year three in seven-year cycle)
Rav Assi / Rabbi Yochanan / Rabbi Yose HaGlili source
